perturbation.original_text: Whereas, VAL wishes to engage the Developer for services as an independent contractor for the sole purpose of designing the Duesey Coffee Chinese Mobile Apps and backend software contained for iPhone, iPad, Android (Hereinafter called as the "Project") developed as per the requirements specifications by VAL within this mobile application development agreement Whereas, the "Developer" is engaged in the making of such applications and holds all the necessary tools to obtain the needed results of this Project for VAL.
perturbation.changed_text: Whereas, VAL wishes to engage the Designer for services as an independent contractor for the sole purpose of designing the Duesey Coffee Chinese Mobile Apps and backend software contained for iPhone, iPad, Android (Hereinafter called as the "Project") developed as per the requirements specifications by VAL within this mobile application development agreement Whereas, the "Developer" is engaged in the making of such applications and holds all the necessary tools to obtain the needed results of this Project for VAL.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Developer" to "Designer" in the first 'Whereas' clause introduces confusion because the subsequent clause still refers to "Developer." This creates uncertainty about who is actually responsible for the Project, since the agreement initially defines the party as 'Developer' but then introduces 'Designer' in the same breath. This introduces a contradiction about who VAL initially wishes to engage with. In section 12.1, for instance, the agreement states, 'VAL shall, in the event of Developer committing any breach', which is contradicted by the opening statement, where the engagement is for the 'Designer', not the 'Developer'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VAL shall, in the event of Developer committing any breach of any of the terms and conditions of this agreement or for any other reason considered as sufficient, be entitled to terminate this agreement by giving two (2) weeks' notice in writing and it is applicable only when the project is not completed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Developer' to 'Designer' introduces a contradiction. The original text consistently refers to 'Developer,' and the contradicted text explicitly mentions 'Developer committing any breach.' By changing the initial reference to 'Designer' while retaining 'Developer' in the subsequent clause and contradicted text, the agreement creates ambiguity about who is responsible for the project, making it unclear who is subject to the breach clause in the contradicted text. This misalignment creates a strong in-text contradiction as it relates to the obligations and responsibilities outlined in the contract, aligning with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS AND OWNERSHIP All Intellectual Property during the project is owned by VAL, and will be turned over to VAL at the conclusion of the project by Developer and after the fulfillment of all commercial obligations by the VAL. All rights and title to Duesey Coffee Intellectual Property created pursuant to the Project shall belong to VAL and shall be subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS AND OWNERSHIP All Intellectual Property during the project is owned by VAL, and will be turned over to VAL at the conclusion of the project by Creator and after the fulfillment of all commercial obligations by the VAL. All rights and title to Duesey Coffee Intellectual Property created pursuant to the Project shall belong to VAL and shall be subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Developer" to "Creator" regarding the intellectual property handover creates a contradiction. The agreement consistently refers to "Developer" elsewhere, implying they are responsible for the project. However, this change suggests the intellectual property handover is the responsibility of the "Creator" not the 'Developer', but the 'Creator' is not properly defined in the original text. In section 6.5, the agreement states, 'Developer will develop the Duesey Coffee Mini Program', which is contradicted since the handover is under the responsibility of the 'Creator', and not the 'Developer'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Developer will develop the Duesey Coffee Mini Program which incorporates the website, products storefront, inventory, ordering systems and payment system. Developer shall ensure the app is technically compliant to WeChat's App guidelines, and VAL's business compliance.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Developer" to "Creator" introduces a contradiction. The original text and the contradicted text clearly identify the 'Developer' as the party responsible for developing the Duesey Coffee Mini Program, including its technical and business compliance (Developer will develop the Duesey Coffee Mini Program which incorporates the website...). The changed text reassigns intellectual property handover to 'Creator', a term undefined in the agreement, creating confusion and contradicting the developer's pre-existing responsibility (All Intellectual Property during the project is owned by VAL, and will be turned over to VAL at the conclusion of the project by Creator...). This creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear who is responsible for delivering the intellectual property at the project's conclusion.

perturbation.original_text: ENGAGEMENT PROCESS & MILESTONE The Engagement Process and Milestone corresponding for this Fixed Price Project Agreement are as follows: Graphic Design/ UI/ Creative Design/ multimedia The VAL is responsible for, and will supply any graphics/ design/ artwork/ multimedia (sound/ video) required for the project to the Developer at either the beginning of the project, or partially during the development. Developer will however put its recommendations; assist in graphic creation for the betterment of the App.
perturbation.changed_text: ENGAGEMENT PROCESS & MILESTONE The Engagement Process and Milestone corresponding for this Fixed Price Project Agreement are as follows: Graphic Design/ UI/ Creative Design/ multimedia The VAL is responsible for, and will supply any graphics/ design/ artwork/ multimedia (sound/ video) required for the project to the Contractor at either the beginning of the project, or partially during the development. Developer will however put its recommendations; assist in graphic creation for the betterment of the App.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Developer" to "Contractor" creates confusion about responsibilities. VAL supplies material to the "Contractor," but the "Developer" still assists with graphic creation. It's unclear if "Contractor" and "Developer" are the same. This introduces ambiguity about whose role is 'to assist in graphic creation'. This is especially in contradiction with section 12.2, where the contractor is responsible of handing over the IPR, or work that is being done.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case Developer terminates the agreement, it shall handover the entire project related IPR, work done till date to VAL.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Developer" to "Contractor" in the modified text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text clearly assigns responsibility for handing over project-related IPR and work done to the 'Developer.' By changing 'Developer' to 'Contractor' in the modified text, while still referencing 'Developer' in the same section concerning assistance with graphic creation, ambiguity arises about who is responsible for the IPR handover as stated in contradicted text, and whether they are the same entity. This ambiguity directly conflicts with the contradicted text which only mentions Developer. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists, per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
